description: Taxonomic classification tasks requiring category assignment. Tests whether E-Prime degrades performance because classification natively relies on 'X is a Y' identity statements.
instructions: Read the following description carefully. Determine which category the described item belongs to. Explain your classification reasoning step by step, then select the best answer.

items.description: A tomato: It grows on a vine, develops from the ovary of a flowering plant, contains seeds inside its fleshy body, and starts green before ripening to red.
items.question: Botanically, a tomato belongs to which category?
items.note: Common misconception — tests whether model follows botanical criteria over culinary convention

items.description: A whale: It breathes air through lungs, gives live birth, nurses its young with milk, and maintains constant body temperature regardless of ocean water temperature.
items.question: A whale belongs to which taxonomic class?

items.description: A function that takes another function as an argument, applies a transformation to every element of a collection, and returns a new collection of the same size.
items.question: This operation pattern classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A system where multiple buyers and sellers interact, prices adjust based on supply and demand, there are low barriers to entry, and no single participant can control the price.
items.question: This describes which market structure?

items.description: An argument that concludes 'We should not legalize X because if we do, Y and Z will inevitably follow, leading to total societal collapse.' It provides no evidence for the causal chain from X to collapse, treating each step as inevitable.
items.question: This argument exemplifies which logical fallacy?

items.description: A polygon with four sides, all sides equal in length, and all interior angles measuring 90 degrees.
items.question: This shape classifies as which of the following?
items.note: Tests most specific classification — all options are technically correct supertypes, but C captures all stated properties

items.description: A piece of music in 3/4 time, with an emphasis on the first beat of each measure, a moderate tempo (~120 BPM), and a flowing melody designed for paired dancing in a ballroom.
items.question: This describes which musical form?
items.note: Minuet is also 3/4 but slower (~80 BPM). Mazurka emphasizes beat 2 or 3, not 1.

items.description: A rock formed from cooled and solidified magma or lava. It may contain visible crystals if it cooled slowly underground, or appear glassy if it cooled rapidly at the surface.
items.question: This rock classifies as which type?

items.description: A government system where power resides in elected representatives, there exists a constitution limiting government authority, the head of state serves a fixed term, and the legislature operates independently from the executive.
items.question: This system of government classifies as which of the following?
items.note: The fixed term + independent legislature distinguishes republic from parliamentary system

items.description: A software testing approach where tests run against the external behavior of a system without knowledge of internal implementation. Tests verify inputs produce expected outputs through the public API.
items.question: This testing approach classifies as which methodology?

items.description: A literary work set in a future society characterized by environmental disaster, totalitarian government, extreme poverty, and the suppression of individual freedoms. The protagonist gradually becomes aware of the system's injustice.
items.question: This narrative genre classifies as which of the following?
items.note: Post-apocalyptic implies civilization has already collapsed; dystopian implies an oppressive society still functions

items.description: An economic indicator that measures the total monetary value of all finished goods and services produced within a country's borders during a specific time period.
items.question: This indicator classifies as which of the following?
items.note: GDP vs GNP: 'within borders' is the key distinguishing criterion

items.description: A chemical reaction where a substance combines with oxygen, releases energy in the form of heat and light, and produces oxides as products. It requires an initial activation energy to begin.
items.question: This reaction classifies as which type?
items.note: All combustion involves oxidation, but the heat+light+oxygen combination specifically defines combustion

items.description: A cognitive distortion where a person draws a broad conclusion from a single incident. For example, after failing one exam, concluding 'I always fail at everything and always will.'
items.question: In cognitive behavioral therapy, this distortion classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A design pattern where a class has only one instance, provides a global point of access to that instance, and lazily initializes itself on first use.
items.question: This pattern classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A philosophical position that holds: knowledge requires justified true belief, justification comes only from sensory experience, and no proposition about the external world can be known a priori.
items.question: This epistemological position classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A nutrient that the body needs in large amounts, provides 4 calories per gram, and the body breaks it down into amino acids during digestion.
items.question: This macronutrient classifies as which of the following?

items.description: An organizational structure where employees report to both a functional manager (by skill type) and a project manager (by deliverable). Authority flows along two dimensions simultaneously.
items.question: This organizational structure classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A type of neural network layer that slides a small window across an input, computes dot products between the window weights and local input regions, and produces a feature map that captures local spatial patterns.
items.question: This layer classifies as which of the following?

items.description: A celestial body that orbits a star, has sufficient mass for gravity to make it roughly spherical, and has cleared the neighborhood around its orbit of other debris.
items.question: This body classifies as which of the following?
items.note: The IAU definition — 'cleared the neighborhood' distinguishes planet from dwarf planet
